content:
# Berea Formation

**Summary**: A common sandstone formation used as a standard for core analysis in the oil industry.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016]] used Berea sandstone cores to evaluate the effectiveness of chelating agents.

- [[2024-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2024]] used Berea sandstone samples to model saline aquifers and depleted oil reservoirs.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] used Berea sandstone cores to model scale precipitation.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2024-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2024]] used Berea sandstone samples to model saline aquifers and depleted oil reservoirs.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] used Berea sandstone cores to model scale precipitation.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016]] porosity and permeability measured during core flood

## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Berea samples showed insignificant change in elastic moduli values during stimulation. ([[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016, p. 19]])

- Berean sandstone was utilized for the entire experimental procedure of the study. ([[2024-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2024, p. 49]])

- The core was successfully used to demonstrate the impact of flow rate and temperature on scaling. ([[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004, p. 15]])
